exemplar.title: To what extent does body weight (measured in kilograms) affect the upper body muscular endurance (measured through maximal body weight push ups) of males aged 14 to 15

subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does body weight (measured in kilograms) affect the upper body muscular endurance (measured through maximal body weight push ups) of males aged 14 to 15

subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

strengths: Consistent links to personal tennis experience and curiosity about body-weight effects
Independent recruitment of participants and original material creation
Clear demonstration of initiative in design, data collection, and ethical procedures
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All strand assessments place the work firmly in the top band for personal engagement (clear, sustained personal input, genuine curiosity, significant independent thinking). Thus the full 2 marks are justified.

strengths: Highly focused and relevant research question guiding the study
Thorough awareness of ethical and safety considerations following BERA guidelines
Good range of background sources and clear procedural planning
weaknesses: Methodology has limitations (self-reported counts, clothing weight, lack of randomization)
Background synthesis uneven, lacking deeper physiological explanations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows a fully focused question and excels in ethics/safety (band 5â6), but methodology and background are âmainly appropriate with limitationsâ (band 3â4). Overall performance sits in the upper half of the mid-band, warranting 4 of 6 marks.

strengths: Extensive raw data set of 42 points supports analysis
Correct calculation of correlation coefficient indicating weak relationship
weaknesses: Inconsistent data processing (unit misinterpretation, missing uncertainty on slope)
Little consideration of measurement uncertainty or statistical error
Interpretation contains an internal contradiction regarding hypothesis support
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strong raw data places one strand in the top band, but major shortcomings in uncertainty treatment (band 1â2), inconsistent processing (band 1â2) and incomplete interpretation (band 3â4) pull the overall analysis to the lower mid-band. A score of 2 reflects these mixed strands.

strengths: Identification of realistic improvements (age matching, tennis-specific cohort)
Some methodological awareness in discussion of limitations
weaknesses: Conclusion at points contradicts earlier interpretation
Comparisons to scientific context are surface-level
Suggestions lack depth on how validity would improve
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All four strands reside in the mid-band (some support, some comparison, basic limitations, some suggestions). However, limited depth and occasional contradictions place the evaluation at the lower mid-band, meriting 2 of 6 marks.

strengths: Well-structured report with clear headings and logical flow
Appropriate use of subject terminology with few errors
weaknesses: Minor formatting inconsistencies (unit notation, citation styles)
Occasional grammatical slips and repetitive phrasing
Visual clutter in some tables and figure captions lacking full detail
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is clear and organized, and terminology use is solid (upper mid-band), but persisting minor errors and inconsistencies prevent full marks. A score of 2 out of 4 accurately reflects these strengths and weaknesses.

strandSelections.reasoning: Apart from noting possible clothing mass, no quantitative treatment of instrument uncertainty or statistical error is given. This constitutes only âlittle consideration of uncertaintyâ, placing it in band 1-2 (score 1).
The student references several external studies when framing results, but comparison is surface-level and not fully critiqued. This fits âsome relevant comparisonâ (band 3-4).

Report follows the expected IB structure (Introduction, Method, Analysis, Evaluation) and information is logically ordered. Minor repetition exists but all required components appear, satisfying âwell-structuredâ criteria.

The student proposes realistic improvements (age-matching, recruiting tennis players) but gives limited detail on how these changes would enhance validity. Suggestions are relevant yet not exhaustive, meriting mid band.

The research question is specific (body-weight vs push-up endurance) and tightly defined to a single sex and age bracket; it is measurable and directly guides the methodology. This represents a relevant and fully focused question; slight phrasing issues do not reduce focus, so low end of top band (5) is appropriate.
Limitations section recognises sample characteristics, extraneous variables and measurement weaknesses, demonstrating some methodological awareness though depth is limited. Thus band 3-4.

The procedure is logical and workable; control variables, warm-up, and ethical steps are listed. However, reliance on self-reported push-up counts, weighing with clothes, and absence of randomisation limit rigour. Therefore methodology is âmainly appropriate with limitationsâ, meriting mid band.

The background contains definitions and several research studies that relate BMI or body composition to endurance, but many sources are tangential or lack depth in explaining mechanisms. Citations are present yet the synthesis is uneven. Thus it is âmainly appropriate and relevantâ rather than entirely so.

Most content is relevant to the research question; digressions are limited. Sections remain concise enough to support understanding, positioning the work within the upper band for focus and relevance.

The student explains that the correlation is very weak and that the null hypothesis cannot be rejected, referencing r, rÂ² and p. However, they later mistakenly state the alternate hypothesis is supported, showing incomplete consistency. Hence interpretation is broadly valid but incomplete.
Headings, tables and figures aid readability. Some grammatical errors and figure references are slightly confusing, but overall presentation remains clear with minimal impediments.

The student repeatedly links the study to personal tennis experience, explains curiosity about manipulating body-weight, and designs a study around an accessible school cohort. The work clearly goes beyond following teacher instructions, showing independent recruitment of 42 peers and production of original materials (diagram, tables). This satisfies âclear evidence of personal engagement with significant independent thinking.â

Means, standard deviations, regression equation, r, rÂ² and p-value are calculated with an appropriate graph. Nonetheless gradient units are misinterpreted, and no uncertainty on slope is shown. This is âappropriate but inconsistentâ processing.

The introduction explicitly states why the topic matters to the studentâs tennis performance and how the findings will guide personal training decisions. The justification displays genuine curiosity and makes the personal significance of the investigation unmistakable, fulfilling the top descriptor.
A table of 42 individual data points for both variables is provided, representing a large, relevant and complete set sufficient for analysis and conclusions, satisfying the top band for raw data.

The student shows initiative by planning participant allocation, preparing equipment, producing consent and ethical procedures, and creating original figures. Data collection and processing were organised by the student with minimal teacher input, demonstrating sustained personal input across all stages.

The report discusses BERA guidelines, confidentiality, supervision, warm-up, first-aid and potential risks, showing full awareness of both safety and ethical issues. Minor detail gaps (e.g., consent forms) prevent 6, but coverage fits the upper band.

Subject terms such as âindependent variableâ, âcoefficient of determinationâ and âmuscular enduranceâ are used correctly and referencing conventions are mostly followed. A few stylistic and citation errors prevent full marks but overall usage is appropriate with minimal errors.

A conclusion is directly linked to the statistical findings, but supporting discussion is brief and some phrasing contradicts earlier interpretation. It is relevant with some support, meeting mid band.

annotations.comment: The raw data tableâs dual-header layout causes visual clutter; consider reorganizing into a single continuous list.
Discussion of extraneous variables acknowledges breadth but lacks prioritization by probable influence; rank key confounders.
Identification of motivational bias is insightful, showing reflection on participant psychology and its impact on data.
Proposed improvements to recruit older and tennis-specific participants are realistic and directly linked to the studentâs goals.
Works Cited list shows breadth but citation formats vary; standardize to one style for consistency.
Some online sources lack access dates, reducing traceability; add dates for all digital references.
The research question is clear and measurable, but consider explicitly stating the expected direction of effect to refine focus and reduce ambiguity.
The introduction effectively ties the studentâs personal tennis experience to the research focus, demonstrating genuine curiosity and independent thinking.
The directional hypothesis (H1) lacks justification regarding why a relationship is expected; consider adding biomechanical reasoning to support it.
Ethical adherence to BERA guidelines and confidentiality protocols is thorough and commendable.
Safety measures including warm-up, supervision, and first aid reflect comprehensive preparation and risk mitigation.
Background section compiles relevant studies but the synthesis is uneven; deepen discussion of physiological mechanisms linking body weight to endurance.
Good variety of sources in the background enhances relevance; citations span physiology, sports science, and demographic studies.
The rationale for using push-ups is well justified by muscle group engagement and ease of administration, reinforcing methodological appropriateness.
The control variables table is comprehensive and logically organized, demonstrating strong planning of experimental factors.
Acknowledging clothing weight as a potential source of error is good, but consider quantifying and subtracting average clothing mass.
Listing confounding variables shows awareness, but these factors could be prioritized by likely impact on push-up performance.
Equipment table effectively lists apparatus and purpose, but lacks stated precision of the digital scale; please specify measurement error.
Recognition of uncontrollable fatigue and motivation effects indicates full ethical and safety awareness.
Methodology sequence is clearly itemized, enhancing reproducibility and facilitating peer review.
The consent process is described, but consider appending a sample consent form as proof of rigorous ethical methodology.
Confidentiality approach with anonymized numbers is well implemented to protect participant identity.
Raw data table includes 42 data points, meeting the requirement for sufficient sample size and comprehensive coverage.
Including summary statistics alongside raw data would improve readability and support immediate insight.
Units are inconsistently labeled (âkilogramsâ vs shorthand); standardize unit notation for clarity.
Regression equation $$y=-0.00143x+21.9$$ is clearly presented, facilitating correct interpretation of model parameters.
No uncertainty estimate is given for the slope; include standard error or confidence interval for the gradient to quantify reliability.
Consider presenting a table comparing calculated p-value (0.99) against critical t-values to strengthen statistical evaluation.
The reported $$r=-0.00158$$ value correctly indicates a negligible negative relationship.
Figure 2.0 caption is clear, but ensure axes on the trend-line graph include units and scale markers for full clarity.
Adding confidenceâinterval shading around the regression line would visually communicate uncertainty in predictions.
Interpretation correctly notes the weak correlation, but the conclusion mistakenly supports the alternate hypothesis; review consistency.
Conclusion section contains relevant links to results but contradicts earlier interpretation; align conclusion argument with statistical findings.
Depth of literature comparison is limited in the conclusion; critically evaluate how each external studyâs context matches your cohort.
Strengths list clearly highlights sample size, controlled environment, and test simplicity, demonstrating methodological awareness.
Limitation regarding age range is well noted; consider a subgroup analysis or statistical adjustment to illustrate impact on results.
